_provenance.stage: Stage 1 — derived from live cards for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: Malaysia Has 2.5 Million Documented Migrant Workers, and Persistent Tier 2 Trafficking Concerns
context: ILO data placed registered migrant workers in Malaysia at ~2.5 million in August 2024. The US 2025 TIP Report kept Malaysia at Tier 2, citing forced-labour concerns in palm oil and gloves.

cards.text: Malaysia hosts ~2.5 million documented migrant workers. The US TIP Report keeps flagging the same forced-labour patterns.
cards.sub: ILO 2024 figure. Malaysia retained Tier 2 status in the 2025 TIP Report, with the State Department citing weak prosecution in palm oil and disposable glove manufacturing.

cards.h: Employment Act 1955 amendments in 2022 extended core protections to foreign workers. Enforcement remains the gap.
cards.s: Forced-labour indicators (passport confiscation, wage theft, debt bondage) appear across sectors. Enforcement targets individual workplaces, not systemic chains.
cards.d: Employment Act 1955 amendments in 2022 extended core protections to foreign workers. Enforcement remains the gap. Forced-labour indicators (passport confiscation, wage theft, debt bondage) appear across sectors. Enforcement targets individual workplaces, not systemic chains.

cards.h: The US TIP Report's Tier 2 status reflects increasing efforts but unmet minimum standards in several key areas.
cards.s: The 2025 report noted fewer trafficker convictions and no labour-trafficking prosecutions in palm oil or glove manufacturing despite ongoing reports.
cards.d: The US TIP Report's Tier 2 status reflects increasing efforts but unmet minimum standards in several key areas. The 2025 report noted fewer trafficker convictions and no labour-trafficking prosecutions in palm oil or glove manufacturing despite ongoing reports.

cards.h: Migrant workers anchor palm oil, electronics assembly, construction and plantations: the export base.
cards.s: Their total economic contribution is not aggregated in any single published figure. Main source countries: Bangladesh, Indonesia, Nepal, Myanmar.
cards.d: Migrant workers anchor palm oil, electronics assembly, construction and plantations: the export base. Their total economic contribution is not aggregated in any single published figure. Main source countries: Bangladesh, Indonesia, Nepal, Myanmar.

cards.text: Tier 2 is not a passing grade. It is a multi-year warning that the system documented in successive TIP reports has not been structurally fixed.
Like a factory that treats its workers' welfare as a compliance line item: essential to hire, not to protect.
cards.sub: Documentation buys access to labour but not enforcement of the protections already in law.

cards.text: MOUs and the 2022 Employment Act amendments are necessary but not sufficient. Tier 1 requires sectoral prosecutions in palm oil and gloves.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
